discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day 9 of steady state. Both sites remain unindexed. Key change this run: SOCIAL-NOTE-003 confirms GSC verification and repo metadata are DONE. Only Bing Webmaster Tools remains pending. Auto-detection signals now verified: google0fa12662dfa239fc.html present in docs/, GitHub API confirms description + 6 topics + homepage URL set. Our repo now has a meaningful SEO advantage over the dome repo (which has null description, zero topics, no homepage URL). The indexing clock is running — GSC verification was 4 days ago (2026-04-12); Google typically begins crawling within 1-2 weeks of verification + sitemap submission. Dome machine-readable layer unchanged: ai_manifest.json V51.1 (69 confirmed, 94.5%), sitemap (22 URLs, lastmod 2026-04-08).

actions_taken.action: Consumed SOCIAL-NOTE-003 and implemented auto-detection checks
actions_taken.reasoning: NOTE-003 directed social to stop flagging GSC and repo metadata as pending, and to auto-detect completion signals. This run now checks: (1) presence of docs/google*.html for GSC, (2) BingSiteAuth.xml for Bing, (3) GitHub API for repo metadata. All three checks executed successfully and will be repeated each run.

human_decisions_needed.question: Bing Webmaster Tools / IndexNow setup
human_decisions_needed.context: The only remaining indexing infrastructure task from SOCIAL-NOTE-002. No BingSiteAuth.xml in docs/, no msvalidate meta tag detected. Lower priority than GSC (which is done) but still worthwhile since DuckDuckGo uses the Bing index. Counterargument: Google is the dominant search engine and GSC is already verified — Bing adds marginal value. However, the marginal effort is very low (sign up, verify, submit sitemap — ~15 minutes), so the cost-benefit still favors doing it.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 22 URLs in sitemap; we have 2
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our site is a single-page review with an llms.txt companion. The dome has 10+ HTML pages, 7 API endpoints, and 3 data files. More sitemap URLs would require more pages, which we don't need — our single-page + progressive-disclosure structure is by design. A sitemap with 2 genuine URLs is better than one padded with fragments.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json with agent_rules and steelman_ladder directing LLMs
no_action_gaps.reasoning: AI-steering, not discoverability. Unchanged assessment. Replicating this would compromise our credibility.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 12 structured API/JSON/CSV endpoints; we have none
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our ClaimReview JSON-LD serves the same purpose for legitimate consumers. No identified consumer would benefit from additional structured JSON endpoints given our narrative-review nature. Unchanged.

search_monitoring.trend_summary: Day 9. Both sites remain absent from all search indices. The key development is that GSC verification completed 4 days ago (April 12). Google's crawl-to-index pipeline typically takes 1-2 weeks after verification + sitemap submission, so we may begin to see indexing in the next 3-10 days assuming the human submits the sitemap via Search Console. The dome's parallel search vacuum continues — their repo has no description, no topics, no homepage link, giving us a structural advantage when indexing does begin. The dome's keyword space remains crowded: 'ECM' collides with automotive (Engine Control Module), 'aetheric' collides with Einstein-aether field theory, 'firmament' is saturated by ANIMA Framework and Journal of Geocentric Cosmology. Our rebrand-resistant anchors (author identity, repo name, specific physics claims) remain the most distinctive search handles.

files_verified_ok: docs/llms.txt — accurately describes 69 dome claims (V51.1) plus sub-entries, methodology and key findings. Wording '69 dome claims plus additional sub-entries' is accurate (70 wins.json entries = 69 numbered WINs + WIN-058b sub-entry).

docs/index.html <head> — meta description template uses ${counts.total} (dynamic), but current deployed HTML shows '69 claims evaluated' because the build predates WIN-058b addition. The build template is correct; the stale HTML will self-correct on next build. ISS-834 already tracks the hardcoded '69' in SEC-1.1 TLDR.
ClaimReview JSON-LD present; references dome's 94.5% accuracy claim, matching current dome ai_manifest.json.

Keywords meta tag includes rebrand-resistant anchors (dome model, dome cosmology, firmament resonance, aetheric medium, Nicholas Hughes, john09289, aetheric refraction index, local sun cosmology). ISS-710 tracks further keyword expansion.
